title: Eyes on arts: Upcoming entertainment highlights
bodyText:
GEORGE ENTERTAINMENT NEWS - Georgians can pick and choose their weekend entertainment from a large variety of events.Below is our weekly calendar for the arts: Eyes on arts.These entries are sent to George Herald on a weekly basis.Have a look and book your tickets for some stunning entertainment on your doorstep.


> 5 - 6 September:


Little Women at the George Arts Theatre. Experience this timeless classic as the March sisters, Meg, Jo, Amy and Beth, navigate life, love and growing up during the American Civil War. A heartfelt journey for all generations. Tickets available on wwwgeorgeartstheatre.co.za.


> 5 September (19:00) and 7 September (16:00):


The Ugly Noo Noo at the Market Community Theatre. A physical theatre play by Andrew Buckland, who recently retired to Knysna. "Buckland's extraordinary performance skills and the power of the script make this an ummissable theatre event that will make the audience howl with laughter at the sheer sensual pleasure of theatrical performance." - Barry Ronge. Tickets are R150 on Webtickets. Bulk bookings or pensioner's discount at R130 per ticket: Heather on 072 369 3304.


> 6 September:


90 Day Comedian (19:00) at the Market Community Theatre with Dalin Oliver. Fresh from performing at the prestigious Melbourne International Comedy Festival, Oliver cannot wait to dive into themes of identity, culture and belonging - offering a hilarious yet moving reflection on what it means to be proudly South African. Tickets at Quicket for R150. Bulk bookings or pensioner's discount at R130 per ticket: Heather on 072 369 3304.


> 12 to 14 September:


Shakespeare Schools Festival (18:00 - 20:00) at the Market Community Theatre. Enjoy performances by two or three schools, with learners presenting their own 30-minute adaptations of Shakespeare's plays, in any language, in a setting that speaks to them. Tickets: Webtickets or at the theatre for R70 and R50.


> Scenes from the Opera, 26 September (18:30) at Sedgefield NG Church and 28 September (16:00) at Market Community Theatre.


Presented by Goldblatt Music Productions, featuring Phyllis Goldblatt (soprano), Siphesihle Mdena (tenor), Luvo Rasemeni (bass-baritone) and Zolina Ngejane (mezzosoprano), with piano accompaniment by Vanessa Phillips. Tickets are R160 and available at the Sedgefield NG Church Office or at the door, and from Quicket or Heather on 072 369 3304 for the production in George.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
